A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION
ATTOIINEYS
T. BRENT HAWKINS

555 CAPITOL MALL, SUTTE 95O
JAll ll 
2 SU fr{ t$2 
sAcRAMENro, .AL,F.RN,A esa,4
(9r6) 444-3900
TELECOPIER: {916) 444-4334
]anuary 24,1992
Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
4281 Street
Sacramento, California 95814
Re: Request For Formal Written Advice: City of Half Moon Bay
Dear Mr. Hallabrin:
The purpose of this letter is to request on behalf of the City of HaIf
Moon Bay and three of its City Councilpersons formal written advice
pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b).
The City of Half Moon Bay (a city of under 10,000 residents) has
undertaken the preparation of a redevelopment plan for the North
Wavecrest Redevelopment Project. The project consists of three contiguous
sub-areas. The southernmost sub-area is what I will refer to as 'North
Wavecrest proper." This is a roughly 500-acre area which is overlaid with
several obsolete paper subdivisions and is currently undeveloped. Adjacent
to the north is the area referred to as "Arleta Park," a residential
neighborhood that has been largely developed. At the northernmost end of
the proposed redevelopment project is the City's downtown area. The
principal focus of the proposed redevelopment project will be the
development of North Wavecrest proper for residential and recreational
uses. The Arleta Park neighborhood and the downtown area would also
benefit from the construction of needed public improvements in those Eueas.
ROSEVILLE 
OFFICE
2260 OOUGLAS BOULEVARO, SUITE 2OO
ROSEVI LLE, CALIFORNIA 9566I
(9t6) 77 3-?att
TELEcoPIER: (916) 773-Oa95
YUBA CITY OFFICE
I585 BUTTE HOUSE ROAO
YUBA CITY. CALIFORNIA 95993
(9t6) 674-9761
TELeCOPTER (916) 671-0990


Scott Hallabrin
fanuary 24,1,992
Page2
Redevelopment plan adoption proceedings have been underway for
some time on the proposed North Wavecrest Redevelopment Project, but
progress has been slow due to limitations on funding. To date, a preliminary
plan has been adopted pursuant to Health and Safety Code sections 33322-
33324, but that is as far as the process has gone. In 1991, the Community
Development Agency ( the City's redevelopment agency) negotiated an
agreement with several of the major property owners in North Wavecrest
proper. (The members of the City Council also sit as the members of the
Community Development Agency.) These property owners agreed to loan
the Community Development Agency over $500,000 to pay the cost of expert
consultants hired by the Agency to prepare the reports and other documents
and studies necessary to complete the redevelopment plan adoption Process.
As part of this agreement, the Agency has agreed to cause a specific plan to be
prepared for North Wavecrest proper, concurrently with the preparation of
the redevelopment plan.
The Redevelopment Plan for the North Wavecrest Redevelopment
Project will cover the entire redeveloPment project area, including North
Wavecrest proper, Arleta Park and the downtown area. As is the general
practice in the redevelopment field, it will be a general document,
functioning more like a charter than a plan. The redevelopment plan
(adopted pursuant to the Community RedeveloPment Law, Health & Saf.
Code 933000 et seq.) wi'll describe the boundaries of the project area, define the
powers of the Agency within the project area (e.g., property acquisition and
disposition, tax increment financing, etc.) and establish some general land
uses. It will not contain detailed provisions governing the use and
development of property, but will rely on and refer to the City' s coastal plan,
zoning and other land use controls. A copy of the Redevelopment Plan for
the South Wavecrest Redevelopment Project, adopted in 1.991, is enclosed for
your reference. It is anticipated that the Redevelopment Plan for the North
Wavecrest Redevelopment Project will be similar.
By contrast, the North Wavecrest Specific Plan (adopted Pursuant to
the Planning and Zoning law, Government Code $65450 ef seq.) will apply
only to North Wavecrest proper. It will contain a specific statement of the
permitted uses, intensities, design criteria, the layout of streets, etc., applicable
only in North Wavecrest proper.
Councilpersons Deborah Ruddock and David Iverson each own real
property in the Arleta Park neighborhood on which their resPective homes


Scott Hallabrin
fanuary 24, 1,992
Page 3
are located. Ms. Ruddock and Mr. Iverson's homes are both located
approximately 600 feet from the northernmost point of North Wavecrest
proper. Councilman Stan Pastorino is an officer and shareholder in a family
corporation which owns a 40-acre parcel of property within the downtown
sub-area. The property is currently unimproved and used for the cultivation
of field flowers. It is located approximately 3500 feet from North Wavecrest
ProPer.
Pursuant to your advice (your file No. A-9G067, dated February 26,
1990), Councilman Iverson has refrained from participating in decisions
relating to the redevelopment plan adoption. Councilpersons Ruddock and
Pastorino were elected to the City Council in November, 1991. They have
been informed that they also have a conflict of interest on decisions
pertaining to the adoption of the redevelopment plan. Since it has now been
determined that a specific plan for North Wavecrest proper will be prepared
concurrently with the preparation of the redevelopment plan for the entire
North Wavecrest Redevelopment Project, Ms. Ruddock, Mr. Pastorino and
Mr. Iverson have asked whether they may participate, as members of the City
Council, in decisions relating only to the North Wavecrest Specific Plan. For
the purposes of your analysis, you should assume that while the preparation
of the two plans will proceed concurently, the redevelopment plan will be
adopted prior to adoption of the specific plan.
Analyzed purely as a zoning decision, the preparation of the North
Wavecrest Specific Plan would not appear to involve a conflict of interest for
Councilpersons Ruddock and Iverson. Under Title 2, California Code of
Regulations, section 18703.1 it seems clear that the effect of any decision
relating to the specific plan on their residences would not be distinguishable
from the effect on the public generally. Half Moon Bay has a population of
under 25,000 and covers a geographic area of less than 10 square miles.
Members of the City Council are required to reside in the City of Half Moon
Bay and are elected at{arge. A decision pertaining to the specific plan would
not have a direct effect on their residences within the meaning of Title 2,
California Code of Regulations, section 18702.2(a)(3) since the the area covered
by the specific plan will not include their residences. The lots on which their
homes are located are approximately 600 feet from the nearest boundary of
the specific plan and are average in size for the community.
For Councilman Pastorino, it would seem that under Title 2, California
Code of Regulations, section 18702.3(b) a decision relating to the specific plan


Scott Hallabrin
January 24,7992
Page 4
would not have a material impact on the property of the family corporation.
That section states that where the property is located more than 2,500 feet
from the property which is the subject of the decision, the decision will not
have a material effect on the property of the decisionmaker unless there are
specific chcumstances regarding the decision, its effect and the nature of the
real property in which the decisionmaker has an interest which make it
reasonably foreseeable that the fair market value of the property will be
affected by more than $10,000. Here, the distance between the family
corporadon's property and North Wavecrest proper is so great that it does not
appear to be reasonably foreseeable that land use decisions pertaining to
North Wavecrest proper would have any effect on the value of the family
corporation's property. We are not aware of any special circumstances which
would lead to a contrary conclusion.
If, purely as a zoning matter, decisions pertaining to the adoption of the
North Wavecrest Specific PIan would not Present conflicts of interest for
Ms. Ruddock, Mr. Pastorino and Mr. Iverson, there nevertheless remains a
question because of the interrelationship of the specific plan and the
redevelopment plan. The specific plan, in addition to being a means of
implementing the City's General Plan, will also be a means of implementing
the Redevelopment Plan for the North Wavecrest Redevelopment Project. It
will provide more specific development criteria, design standards and
environmental mitigations for a portion of the redeveloPment Project area.
It would appear, however, that under criteria applied by the FPPC in the past
to redevelopment plan implementation decisions and general plan decisions,
Ms. Ruddock, Mr. Pastorino and Mr. Iverson would not necessarily have a
conflict of interest, so long as the redeveloPment plan is adopted first. I refer
to advice letters like the letter to Stuart Scudder, dated ]une 20, 1988 (File
No. 4-88-181) and Michael H. Rousch, dated December Z 1988 (File
No. 4-88-404) which have advised that redeveloPment plan implementing
decisions must be analyzed on an individual basis for conflicts of interest and
that a decisionmaker does not necessarily have a conflict of interest on an
implementation decision even though he or she may have had a conflict of
interest on the decision to adoPt the redevelopment Plan. The same general
principle is involved in advice letters like those to Raymond M. Haight, dated
f anuary 23, 79t36 (File No. A-t36-027); Mary R. Casey, dated December 37, 19136
(File No. I-86-310); Thomas Angelo, dated September 2,'l'987 (File
No. I-87-217); and ]effery D. Huffaker, dated January 9, 1987 (File No. A-85-343)
which involve participation in the decision to enact a general Plan where a


Scott Hallabrin
]anuary 24, 1992
Page 5
decisionmaker has not participated in a decision about an individual element
of the general plan due to a conflict of interest on that element.
Please provide us with your opinion on whether Councilpersons
Ruddock, Pastorino and Iverson may participate in decisions pertaining to the
North Wavecrest Specific PIan. They would, of course, continue to disqualify
themselves from participating in decisions relating only to the adoption of
the North Wavecrest Redevelopment Plan. I am available to answer any
further questions you may have.
Very truly yours,
T. Brent Hawkins
rBH/kh
cc: Mark Weiss, City Manager
Robert Lanzone, City Attorney
Councilwoman Deborah Ruddock
Councilman David Iverson
Councilman Stan Pastorino
]ames K. Burns
Enclosure
